examples.english: I was born in a big city.
He neither begets nor is born,

senses.gloss: to assist in childbirth (as a midwife)
to make children to, to cause to give birth
to rear, to educate, to bring up
to innovate, to originate, to generate, to produce

examples.text: وَجَمَالُ ٱلْأَشْخَاصِ هُوَ أَمْرٌ يُؤَثِّرُ بِٱلْمَشَاعِرِ وَيُوَلِّدُ ٱلْإنْجِذَابَ نَحْوَ الْآخَرِينَ.
examples.english: The beauty of people is something that affects feelings and generates attraction towards others.

senses.gloss: child (“son or daughter of any age”)

examples.english: I want to play with your children.

examples.text: وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلَادَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلَاقٍ ۖ نَحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ قَتْلَهُمْ كَانَ خِطْئًا كَبِيرًا
examples.english: And do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We provide for them and for you. Indeed, their killing is ever a great sin.

etymology.text:
From Proto-Semitic *walad-.

Form-II intensive/causative of وَلَدَ (walada, “to give birth”).

Compare Hebrew יֶלֶד (yéled), Ge'ez ወልድ (wäld).
